description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular walkie-talkie or two-way radio with a long, cylindrical antenna extending from the top. The front face features a prominent speaker grille, two round buttons, and a smaller knob, likely for power or volume control. The side view reveals a single large button, typically used for push-to-talk functionality, and a small protruding switch or clip. The back is plain except for a raised circular section, possibly a battery compartment or clip attachment. The casing appears to be made of dark blue or green plastic with black accents, giving it a rugged, utilitarian look suitable for outdoor or urban use. The overall design is simple, functional, and built for handheld operation.
description: A compact, rectangular walkie-talkie with a long antenna, speaker grille, and multiple control buttons. The casing is dark blue-green plastic with black accents, designed for handheld use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, two round buttons, and a small knob below the antenna.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a large round push-to-talk button and a small protruding switch or clip.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain surface and a raised circular section, likely for battery or clip attachment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mostly featureless except for the protruding switch or clip visible from the side.
